title: End date must be on or after start date and not in the future
description: When a form has a paired *STDAT / *ENDAT field set (e.g. MHSTDAT+MHENDAT, AESTDAT+AEENDAT, CMSTDAT+CMENDAT), the end-date field gets a constraint `. >= ${<base>STDAT} and . <= today()` and a constraint_message explaining the rule. Sibling lookup via field.has_sibling in same_itemgroup scope.
rationale: Free-text end dates that are before the matched start date, or in the future, are almost always typos or data-entry slips. The paired-date constraint is one of the most common clinical-trial data-quality rules — present on essentially every form that captures temporal intervals (AEs, concomitant medications, medical history, deviations, etc.).

flag.message: Field ${field.name} is an end-date paired with a sibling start-date in the same itemgroup. Verify constraint is `. >= ${<sibling>STDAT} and . <= today()` and constraint_message reads 'End date must be on or after start date and cannot be in the future.'

effect.soft: Emit constraint `. >= ${<base>STDAT} and . <= today()` where <base> is the field-name prefix shared by the STDAT sibling (e.g. for AEENDAT, the sibling is AESTDAT and the constraint references ${AESTDAT}). Emit constraint_message: 'End date must be on or after start date and cannot be in the future.' Constraint expression composition from sibling name requires string interpolation the DSL does not yet support — handled here in soft.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7A during Phase B.1c-final. Uses field.has_sibling DSL operator from B.1c-1 (commit c9ef634). Hybrid because constraint expression must reference the sibling's actual field name (interpolation of <base>STDAT from <base>ENDAT requires string manipulation not yet in the DSL). When DSL grows a sibling-value interpolation operator, this becomes structured.
